summary: Use when a caller reports an adult collapsed, unconscious, not breathing normally, gasping, or requiring CPR. Dispatcher should confirm location, check breathing, dispatch ambulance immediately, and guide caller through CPR if appropriate.

Defines scope and governance for the MERS-AI voice agent. The AI operates as a decision-support tool only, must not override human dispatchers, must not claim dispatch without CAD confirmation, and must escalate to human when SOP confidence is low.

Defines the standard call-taking flow for all emergencies: answer and identify, lock location first, obtain callback number, identify chief complaint, screen life threats, dispatch early for P1/P2, continue key questions, provide pre-arrival instructions, keep caller on line, update responders and close safely.

skill_name: Dispatcher Tone, Language and Caller Control
summary: Guides dispatcher communication style: calm, firm, empathetic tone with short sentences, one question at a time, bilingual Malay/English support, caller control techniques like repetitive persistence and bridging, and dignity preservation.

skill_name: Minimum Data Set for Every Emergency Incident
summary: Specifies required data fields for every incident record including location, callback, category, priority, patient status, hazards, responder recommendations, SOP IDs retrieved and confidence scores. Unknown fields should be recorded as unknown rather than hallucinated.

Defines transparent priority scale: P1 immediate life threat, P2 emergency, P3 urgent, P4 non-urgent and MCI mode for mass casualty. Priority is dynamic and must upgrade immediately when life-threatening changes occur.

Structures responder handoff using SBAR format: Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation. Includes example CAD/radio summary. Handoff must use caller-reported facts, avoid unverified diagnosis and update whenever priority-changing facts emerge.

skill_name: How the SOP RAG Agent Should Retrieve and Answer
summary: Defines RAG retrieval process: query expansion, hybrid search, reranking by operational relevance, primary plus fallback SOP selection, confidence flagging, and guardrails for location-first behaviour, cardiac arrest priority, scene safety and child/silent callers.

skill_name: Severe Breathing Difficulty / Asthma / Respiratory Distress
summary: Use when caller reports shortness of breath, asthma attack, wheezing, blue lips, choking sensation, or allergic reaction. Keep patient upright, escalate to P1 if unable to speak or turning blue, assist with prescribed medication only, and monitor for deterioration to cardiac arrest.

Use when caller reports chest pain, pressure, tightness, pain radiating to arm/jaw/back, sweating, nausea or suspected heart attack. Stop patient activity, monitor for cardiac arrest, dispatch ambulance and avoid private transport for severe chest pain.

Use when caller reports face droop, slurred speech, weak arm, sudden weakness or confusion. Record last-known-well time, keep patient resting, no food or drink, dispatch time-sensitive ambulance and flag suspected stroke for stroke-capable pathway.

Use when caller reports choking, food stuck, inability to breathe or speak, or person turning blue. Differentiate partial from severe obstruction, guide back blows and abdominal thrusts for adults, retrieve infant-specific protocol for babies, and switch to CPR if unconscious.

Use when caller reports heavy bleeding, blood pumping, amputation, deep cut, stab wound, or gunshot wound. Guide direct pressure with cloth, do not remove embedded objects, dispatch ambulance and add police if violence involved.

skill_name: Road Traffic Collision / Motor Vehicle Crash
summary: Use when caller reports car accident, motorbike crash, lorry collision, hit pedestrian, trapped victim, or roadside bleeding. Secure location, identify hazards, coordinate ambulance/police/Bomba, and instruct caller not to move casualties unless immediate danger.

skill_name: Fall / Head Injury / Suspected Spinal Injury
summary: Use when caller reports a fall, head impact, unconsciousness after fall, neck pain, inability to move, or elderly fall. Do not move patient unless immediate danger, monitor consciousness and breathing, and dispatch ambulance with Bomba for height/confined rescue.

Use when caller reports seizure, fit, convulsion, jerking, foaming, or epileptic episode. Protect from injury, time the seizure, do not restrain or put objects in mouth, monitor breathing after seizure stops, and switch to CPR if not breathing normally.

Use when caller reports overdose, poison ingestion, chemical swallowing, carbon monoxide, gas leak, or pesticide exposure. Protect caller from fumes, do not induce vomiting, keep container for responders, and add hazmat/police as needed.

Use when caller reports burn, scald, person on fire, explosion, chemical burn, electrical burn, or smoke inhalation. Stop burning, cool with water, do not apply home remedies, check airway and breathing, and dispatch ambulance with Bomba for active fire.

Use when caller reports labour, contractions, water breaking, baby coming, or pregnant bleeding. Dispatch ambulance, assess for imminent delivery, keep mother comfortable, do not pull baby or cord, and escalate to human dispatcher for delivery guidance.

Use when caller reports drowning, person fell into water, not breathing after swimming, or flood water rescue. Do not let untrained callers enter water, coordinate rescue/Bomba, start CPR once victim is safely out and not breathing normally.

Use when caller reports building fire, smoke, burning building, trapped persons, or fire alarm/explosion. Instruct evacuation via stairs, stay low under smoke, do not re-enter, and dispatch Bomba with ambulance for injuries and police for crowd control.

skill_name: Hazardous Material / Chemical Spill / Gas Leak
summary: Use when caller reports chemical spill, gas smell, chlorine, ammonia, pesticide, unknown fumes, or lab accident. Move away from substance, do not touch or smell, evacuate upwind, do not switch electrical devices, and dispatch Bomba/hazmat with ambulance.

skill_name: Domestic Violence / Family Violence in Progress
summary: Use when caller reports being hit, family violence, locked in, threatened, or children in danger. Prioritize caller safety, use discreet yes/no questions if abuser nearby, dispatch police urgently, add ambulance for injuries, and do not instruct confrontation.

skill_name: Weapon Threat / Assault / Robbery in Progress
summary: Use when caller reports knife, gun, robbery, being attacked, fight, stabbing, armed suspect, or hostage. Keep caller hidden/safe, gather suspect details without increasing risk, dispatch police with ambulance staged until scene secure.

skill_name: Trapped Person / Building Collapse / Confined Rescue
summary: Use when caller reports trapped person, collapsed building, landslide burial, confined space, or inability to get out. Do not encourage untrained rescue, dispatch Bomba/rescue with ambulance, gather victim location details, and switch to MCI if multiple victims.

Use when caller reports flood, flash flood, landslide, rising water, trapped on roof, mudslide, or evacuation need. Move to higher ground, do not enter moving floodwater, dispatch rescue/Bomba with police for evacuation, and escalate to MCI if widespread.

skill_name: Mass Casualty Incident / Major Incident Mode
summary: Use when caller reports many injured, bus crash, explosion, building collapse, train crash, or mass poisoning. Stop single-patient workflow, establish incident scale and hazards, gather casualty counts by severity, activate multi-agency MCI response.

Use when caller reports suicidal thoughts, wanting to die, self-harm, taking pills, standing on bridge, cutting, or hanging attempt. Use compassionate non-judgmental language, keep person engaged, reduce access to means if safe, and dispatch police/medical response.

skill_name: Silent Call / Open Line / Caller Cannot Speak
summary: Use when dispatcher receives silent call, open line, no speech, background screaming, caller whispering, or hang-up. Use yes/no tap prompts, preserve location data, infer danger from audio context, and escalate for welfare check if distress indicators present.

Use when a child calls emergency services reporting hurt adult, lost child, or emergency they cannot describe clearly. Use simple age-appropriate language, verify location via landmarks, do not dismiss as prank, and prioritize child escape if fire or violence.

skill_name: Lift Entrapment / Confined Person Without Immediate Medical Threat
summary: Use when caller reports being stuck in lift, elevator trapped, or cannot open door. Do not force doors or climb out, press alarm/intercom, dispatch building management and Bomba/rescue, and add ambulance if medical symptoms or vulnerable person.

skill_name: Missing Child / Lost Person High Risk
summary: Use when caller reports missing child, lost kid, abduction suspicion, or wandered off. Capture description, clothing, last-seen time and location, risk factors, and nearby hazards quickly. Dispatch police immediately and do not advise waiting 24 hours.

Use when caller appears to be joking, abusive, making repeated non-emergency calls, or suspected hoax. Maintain professionalism, verify no concealed emergency exists, refer to non-emergency line if appropriate, and document repeat abuse.

skill_name: RAG Chunking Strategy for Single-Document Embeddings
summary: Defines chunking strategy for SOP RAG: split by SOP ID, 600-1200 tokens for incident protocols, 300-700 for universal rules, begin each chunk with SOP ID and tags, and store metadata fields for retrieval filtering.

skill_name: Recommended SOP Agent JSON Output Schema
summary: Defines structured JSON output schema for the SOP RAG Agent including sop_id, confidence, priority, caller instruction, responder handoff, must-not-say list and escalation flag. Designed for low-latency real-time voice call integration.

skill_name: Safety Guardrails for AI Voice Agent
summary: Defines hard stops for the AI agent: no medication dosage without approved SOP, no directing callers into danger, no definitive diagnosis, no false dispatch claims, and mandatory human escalation for low-confidence, rare scenarios, and tactical situations.

skill_name: Testing and Evaluation Plan for SOP RAG
summary: Defines evaluation methodology: 100+ simulated emergency utterances across categories, key metrics including correct SOP retrieval, priority assignment, hallucination rate, unsafe instruction rate, and red-team test cases for edge scenarios.
